message:
security: shared file locks for auth reads and 0o600 permissions for session files

Two complementary security improvements:

1. auth.rs: Switch AuthStorage::load() from exclusive lock to shared
   lock via new lock_file_shared() function. Reading auth.json does
   not need exclusive access — shared locks allow concurrent readers
   while still blocking during exclusive writes. Uses exponential
   backoff with jitter (10ms base, 500ms cap) matching the existing
   lock_file() pattern.

2. session_store_v2.rs: Introduce secure_open_options() helper that
   sets Unix file permissions to 0o600 (owner read/write only) on
   all newly created session files. Applied to 7 file operations:
   segment writes, checkpoint files, manifest files, offset indices,
   JSONL appends, file truncation, and JSONL bulk writes. Prevents
   other users on the system from reading session data.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
